entries.name: Get Waveform Attribute Function
entries.description: Retrieves the names and values of all attributes or the value of a single attribute, depending on whether you wire the name parameter. Attributes can be channel names. The connector pane displays the default data types for this polymorphic function.

terminals.name: waveform
terminals.description: waveform is the waveform for which you want to retrieve an attribute(s) and value(s).

terminals.name: name
terminals.description: name is the name of the attribute whose value you want to retrieve. Do not wire this parameter if you want to retrieve all the attributes associated with the specified waveform. If you wire name, the names output changes to a Boolean output found, the array output values changes to an output called value of the same type as default value, and the function searches for only the specified attribute.
terminals.default_value: value, and the function searches for only the specified attribute

terminals.name: default value (empty Variant)
terminals.description: default value is a value and data type that you specify. If the function does not find the attribute specified in name, it returns the default value. If you wire default value, you must wire name.
terminals.default_value: value is a value and data type that you specify

terminals.name: error in (no error)
terminals.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality.

terminals.name: duplicate waveform
terminals.description: duplicate waveform is the waveform data you input in waveform.

terminals.name: names
terminals.description: Functions 802 ni.com names returns a 1D array containing the names of all attributes associated with the waveform. If you wire the name parameter, this output changes to a Boolean value found. found returns TRUE if the function finds the attribute that you specify in name.

terminals.name: values
terminals.description: values returns a 1D array containing the values associated with each attribute of the specified waveform in variant format. You must unflatten each attribute value to an appropriate data type. If you wire name, this output changes to a single variant value. If the function does not find the attribute that you specify in name, it returns the value passed to default value.

terminals.name: error out
terminals.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. This function has two modes of behavior depending on whether you wire the name parameter. By default, the function returns the names of all attributes and their corresponding values in 1D arrays. If you wire name, the names output changes to a Boolean output found, the values output changes to a variant output value, and the function searches for only the specified attribute. If the function does not fin
terminals.default_value: value, found is FALSE, and value displays the contents of default value

entries.name: Analog to Digital
entries.description: Converts analog waveform to a digital waveform or digital data. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. • DWDT Analog to Digital VI • DTbl Analog to Digital VI To create digital data from an analog waveform or to graph an analog signal in a digital waveform graph, you must convert the raw analog data into digital data or the digital waveform data type. Use the Analog to Digital VI to convert the data to digital waveform. Use the Get Waveform Components function to extr

terminals.name: compress digital (T)
terminals.description: compress digital specifies whether to compress the digital waveform or digital data. The default is TRUE.

terminals.name: analog waveform
terminals.description: analog waveform is the waveform you want to convert to digital waveform or digital data.

terminals.name: resolution (16 bit)
terminals.description: resolution specifies the number of bits represented in the digital waveform or digital data.

terminals.name: full-scale range (pk-pk)
terminals.description: full-scale range (pk-pk) specifies the total peak-to-peak range, or the difference between the minimum and maximum, for the digital waveform or digital data. For example, if the maximum range of a waveform is 1 and the minimum is –1, the full-scale range for the waveform is 2. The default is 2.

terminals.name: error in (no error)
terminals.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. With the following exception, this input provides standard error in functionality. If an error occurred before this node runs, this node outputs an empty waveform.

terminals.name: data format (offset binary)
terminals.description: data format specifies which binary representation you want to use for the digital waveform or digital data. 0 unsigned binary—Converts the data to unsigned binary. 1 offset binary (default)—The largest negative full-scale value is represented by all zeros, and the largest positive full-scale value is represented by all ones. Zero-scale is represented by a one (MSB) followed by all zeros, for example, binary 1000. 2 2's complement—Uses two's complement format, which is a common format for represe

terminals.name: digital waveform
terminals.description: Functions

entries.name: Index Waveform Array
entries.description: Selects one waveform out of an array of analog or digital waveforms by array index or channel name. The data types you wire to the waveform array and index inputs and the data type of the Y component of the analog waveform determine the polymorphic instance to use. • WDT Index Channel DBL VI • WDT Index Channel I64 VI • WDT Index Channel I32 VI • WDT Index Channel I16 VI • WDT Index Channel CDB VI • WDT Index Channel by Name DBL VI • WDT Index Channel by Name I64 VI • WDT Index Channel by Name I

terminals.name: waveform array
terminals.description: waveform array is an array of analog waveforms from which to extract a single waveform.

terminals.name: index
terminals.description: index is the index of the waveform to extract from waveform array.

terminals.name: error in (no error)
terminals.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality.

terminals.name: waveform
terminals.description: waveform is the analog waveform selected from the array of waveforms.

terminals.name: error out
terminals.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. Examples Refer to the following example files included with LabVIEW.

entries.name: Copy Waveform dt
entries.description: Replaces all dt values with the dt value of the waveform at index position in waveforms in. The data type you wire to the waveforms in input and the data type of the Y component of the analog waveform determine the polymorphic instance to use. • WDT Copy Waveform dt DBL VI • WDT Copy Waveform dt I64 VI • WDT Copy Waveform dt I32 VI • WDT Copy Waveform dt I16 VI • WDT Copy Waveform dt CDB VI • WDT Copy Waveform dt EXT VI • WDT Copy Waveform dt CXT VI • DWDT Copy Waveform dt VI WDT Copy Waveform d

terminals.name: waveforms in
terminals.description: waveforms in contains the analog waveforms for which the time increment values are replaced by the time increment of the analog waveform indicated by index. The default is empty.

terminals.name: index
terminals.description: Functions 828 ni.com index is the specifier that the waveform in the waveforms in array uses for the dt value. The default is 0.

terminals.name: error in (no error)
terminals.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality.

terminals.name: waveforms out
terminals.description: waveforms out contains analog waveforms for which the dt values are all identical.

terminals.name: error out
terminals.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality.

entries.name: WDT Copy Waveform dt DBL VI

entries.name: Align Waveform Timestamps
entries.description: Replaces all the time stamp values (t0) in waveforms in with the value of the index element in the array. The data type you wire to the waveforms in input and the data type of the Y component of the analog waveform determine the polymorphic instance to use. • WDT Align Waveform Timestamps DBL VI • WDT Align Waveform Timestamps I64 VI • WDT Align Waveform Timestamps I32 VI • WDT Align Waveform Timestamps I16 VI • WDT Align Waveform Timestamps CDB VI • WDT Align Waveform Timestamps EXT VI • WDT Al

terminals.name: waveforms in
terminals.description: waveforms in contains all the analog waveforms for which time stamps (t0) are to be changed.

terminals.name: index
terminals.description: index selects which waveform to extract the time stamp from. The time stamps of all elements of waveforms in are changed to this time stamp.

terminals.name: error in (no error)
terminals.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality.

terminals.name: waveforms out
terminals.description: waveforms out contains the waveforms in array with all time stamps on the elements changed to the time stamp of the element indicated by index.

terminals.name: error out
terminals.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. Functions 836 ni.com Examples Refer to the following example files included with LabVIEW.

entries.name: Get Waveform Subset
entries.description: Retrieves a subset of a waveform at a specified time or index. You must manually select the polymorphic instance you want to use. • WDT Get Waveform Subset DBL VI • WDT Get Waveform Subset CDB VI • WDT Get Waveform Subset EXT VI • WDT Get Waveform Subset I16 VI • WDT Get Waveform Subset I32 VI • WDT Get Waveform Subset I8 VI • WDT Get Waveform Subset SGL VI • DWDT Get Waveform Subset VI • DTbl Digital Subset VI For example, consider a waveform of 128 scans with a dt of 1.00. If you wire 40.00 to

terminals.name: open interval? (T)
terminals.description: open interval? defines whether the waveform subset extracted is an open or closed interval. The default is TRUE, which selects an open interval. For example, if t0 = 0, dt = 1, Y = {0, 1, 2}, start/duration format is Relative Time, start samples/ time is 0, and duration is 2, an open interval returns {0, 1}. A closed interval returns {0, 1, 2}.
terminals.default_value: TRUE, which selects an open interval

terminals.name: start/duration format
terminals.description: start/duration format specifies if you want to retrieve the data value of the specified element or at a specified time. 0 Samples (default)—Returns the data value of the specified element in the waveform data. 1 Relative Time—Returns the data value at a specified time relative to the first point in the waveform. In this mode, the dt of the waveform affects actual start samples/time and actual duration.

terminals.name: waveform in
terminals.description: waveform in is the waveform for which you want to retrieve a subset.

terminals.name: start samples/time
terminals.description: start samples/time is the data element or time value where you want to start acquiring a waveform subset.

terminals.name: duration
terminals.description: Functions

A closed interval returns {0, 1, 2}.
terminals.default_value: TRUE, which selects an open interval

In this mode, the dt of the waveform affects actual start samples/time and actual duration.
